macro_summary: S&P 500 futures edged higher Friday after indexes closed April at record highs, with Big Tech earnings delivering a split verdict. The Employment Cost Index for Q1 showed a 3.4% compensation cost increase over 12 months, reinforcing the Fed's late-2026 rate-cut timeline amid persistent wage inflation. WTI slipped 0.3% to $105.09 as the Hormuz crisis remains the dominant supply-risk premium, while the VIX dropped 10% to 16.89, offering tactical calm.
commodities_note: WTI at $105.09, down 0.3% from Thursday's $109.40 spike, but the Strait of Hormuz disruption continues to underpin a $100+ floor. Gold eased 0.15% to $4,624.70, and the US10Y yield dipped 0.63% to 4.39%, suggesting some flight-to-safety unwinding after the BigTech earnings flush.

singapore.summary: The Singapore cluster remains under pressure with all three banks now on HOLD or CAUTION. DBS has been hard-blocked by RSI, while OCBC and UOB downgrade to HOLD ahead of earnings. The key cluster-level signal: until US10Y sustainably breaks below 4.20% or MAS signals easing in October, NIM compression will cap upside.
singapore.thesis_status: NIM compression day 45; rate differential headwind structural, no near-term relief catalyst.
singapore.key_development: OCBC (May 8) and UOB (May 7) report earnings this week — the outcomes will determine whether the cluster can stabilise or requires further defensive positioning.

semiconductors.summary: The Semis cluster is bifurcated: mega-caps NVDA, AMD, INTC, MU, AVGO are all hard-blocked by extreme extensions, while foundry-adjacent ASML and LITE hold WATCH/CAUTION with upcoming earnings as binary catalysts. SOXX rebounded 1% but remains hostage to oil logistics margin pressure and duration headwinds from a 4.39% US10Y.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Bifurcation persists; mid-cap foundry thesis intact but macro margin compression from WTI $105+ adds cost headwinds.
semiconductors.key_development: AMD, LITE, and PLTR all report within 5 days — these earnings will validate whether the AI infrastructure spending supercycle is accelerating or peaking.

big_tech.summary: BigTech delivered a split verdict: GOOG surged post-earnings but is extreme-extension blocked, while MSFT's OpenAI concentration disclosure and AMZN's $200B capex overhang keep both names under caution or just downgraded to WATCH. The cluster's capex ROI debate is now priced into individual names rather than the group, creating a selective setup.
big_tech.thesis_status: Capex moderation validated; MSFT and GOOG are the cleanest stories, but both are technically extended.
big_tech.key_development: MSFT's -4.2% drop brought it within 3% of SMA50, creating a potential WATCH entry if the pullback continues; GOOG remains +22% above SMA50 post-earnings, needing a larger reset.

interconnected.name: Coherent
interconnected.reason: NVDA's $2B photonics investment (alongside LITE) makes COHR a direct read-through for AI datacenter optical interconnects. The story is structural, not a one-quarter trade.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if COHR pulls back 8-10% from current levels, or after LITE's May 6 earnings provide photonics demand clarity.

active_risks: Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed to commercial traffic; insurance markets and shipping lines have not normalised.
US-Iran tensions are escalating with no diplomatic off-ramp, sustaining a $100+ WTI floor and logistics inflation.
China's factory activity expanded for a second month (PMI 52.2), showing resilience despite war-driven energy costs.
Fed rate cut delayed to late 2026 on war-related inflation, keeping duration pressure on mega-cap tech.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil at $105 keeps energy costs elevated, hitting semis supply chains (ASML, TSM) through freight and component inflation. The rate-cut delay punishes long-duration assets, visible in GOOG and AMZN's extreme extensions blocking entry. Singapore banks (D05, O39, U11) face NIM compression from the wide US-SGD rate gap with no near-term relief.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Employment Cost Index Q1 showed 3.4% YoY compensation growth, reinforcing the wage-push inflation narrative that supports the Fed's hawkish stance.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: No new entries into extreme-extended names; wait for pullbacks in WATCH names (ASML, MSFT) and let earnings resolve the binary risk for AMD, LITE, and PLTR.

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: Hormuz oil shock and delayed rate cuts are the dominant risks, pushing all mega-cap semis and BigTech into extreme extension. Entry is only viable in the two WATCH names, and even those require pullbacks or earnings clarity.

macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Fed pushes rate cut to 2027, US10Y stays above 4.39%.
bearish_impact: Singapore banks NIM compression extends; D05_SI, O39_SI, U11_SI remain CAUTION/HOLD.

macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Commercial segment decelerates, Q2 guide below 60% growth.
